note: Rule summaries are original paraphrases for tooling purposes, not the licensed MISRA text. Consult the official MISRA C:2012 document for normative wording.

Dir 4.4.summary: Source code should not be left disabled inside comments.
Dir 4.4.fix: Delete commented-out code; rely on version control history instead.

Dir 4.6.summary: Prefer fixed-width typedefs (int32_t, uint8_t, ...) over basic numeric types.

Dir 4.12.summary: Dynamic memory allocation shall not be used.
Dir 4.12.fix: Use static allocation, memory pools, or stack buffers sized at compile time.

Rule 7.1.summary: Octal constants shall not be used.
Rule 7.1.fix: Rewrite octal literals as decimal or hexadecimal.

Rule 7.3.summary: A lowercase 'l' shall not be used as a literal suffix.
Rule 7.3.fix: Use uppercase 'L' suffix to avoid confusion with digit 1.

Rule 8.4.summary: External objects and functions need a compatible visible declaration before definition.
Rule 8.4.fix: Declare the symbol in a header included by its defining source file.

Rule 8.7.summary: Objects and functions referenced in only one translation unit should not have external linkage.

Rule 10.1.summary: Operands shall have an appropriate essential type for the operator.
Rule 10.1.fix: Avoid mixing signed/unsigned/boolean/char operands; add explicit conversions with range checks.

Rule 11.3.summary: A pointer to one object type shall not be cast to a pointer to a different object type.
Rule 11.3.fix: Redesign with unions of the correct type, memcpy, or proper typed interfaces.

Rule 13.4.summary: The result of an assignment operator should not be used as a value.
Rule 13.4.fix: Split 'if (x = f())' into an assignment statement followed by a comparison.

Rule 14.4.summary: Controlling expressions of if/while shall have essentially Boolean type.

Rule 15.1.summary: The goto statement should not be used.
Rule 15.1.fix: Restructure with loops/early returns; if goto is retained for cleanup, it must only jump forward within the function (15.2/15.3).

Rule 15.5.summary: A function should have a single point of exit at the end.
Rule 15.5.fix: Consolidate returns via a result variable where it does not harm clarity.

Rule 15.6.summary: Bodies of if/else/loop statements shall be compound (brace-enclosed) blocks.
Rule 15.6.fix: Add braces around every controlled statement body.

Rule 16.4.summary: Every switch statement shall have a default label.
Rule 16.4.fix: Add a default clause that handles or asserts on unexpected values.

Rule 17.1.summary: Features of <stdarg.h> shall not be used.
Rule 17.1.fix: Replace variadic interfaces with fixed-arity functions or tables.

Rule 17.2.summary: Functions shall not call themselves directly or indirectly (no recursion).
Rule 17.2.fix: Convert recursion to iteration with an explicit bounded stack.

Rule 17.7.summary: The value returned by a non-void function shall be used or explicitly discarded.
Rule 17.7.fix: Check the return value or cast to (void) with justification.

Rule 18.8.summary: Variable-length arrays shall not be used.
Rule 18.8.fix: Use fixed-size arrays with a documented maximum, or a static pool.

Rule 19.2.summary: The union keyword should not be used.
Rule 19.2.fix: Prefer structs or explicit serialization; if a union is essential, document and deviate.

Rule 20.4.summary: A macro shall not share its name with a language keyword.

Rule 20.5.fix: Scope macros properly instead of undefining them.

Rule 21.3.summary: malloc/calloc/realloc/free from <stdlib.h> shall not be used.
Rule 21.3.fix: Use static allocation or a project-approved pool allocator.

Rule 21.4.fix: Replace non-local jumps with structured error propagation (return codes).

Rule 21.5.fix: Handle asynchronous events through the RTOS/platform mechanism instead of signals.

Rule 21.6.summary: Standard input/output functions (<stdio.h>) shall not be used in production code.
Rule 21.6.fix: Route diagnostics through a project logging layer; guard debug prints behind build flags.

Rule 21.7.fix: Use strtol/strtoul/strtod with full error checking.

Rule 21.8.summary: Termination and environment functions (abort, exit, getenv, system) shall not be used.
Rule 21.8.fix: Report failures through the application's error path; never shell out.

Rule 21.9.summary: bsearch and qsort shall not be used.

Rule 21.10.summary: Date/time facilities of <time.h> shall not be used.
Rule 21.10.fix: Use the platform clock services with defined behavior.
